title: Building Effective Agents - Anthropic Research
core_insight: Effective AI agents require structured workflows with explicit tool use and human oversight, not autonomous black-box systems.
summary: Anthropic's research on building effective AI agents emphasizes the importance of structured agent workflows over autonomous operation. The paper argues for explicit tool use patterns, human-in-the-loop oversight, and clear separation of concerns in agent architectures.
decision_reason: Primary source from leading AI lab with actionable agent design patterns

title: LLM Powered Autonomous Agents - Lil'Log
core_insight: LLM-powered agents combine planning, memory, and tool use into a cognitive architecture that extends beyond simple prompt-response patterns.
summary: Comprehensive survey of LLM agent architectures covering planning strategies (task decomposition, self-reflection), memory types (short-term context, long-term vector store), and tool use patterns. Provides a unified framework for understanding agent design.
decision_reason: Definitive survey of agent architectures with systematic framework and extensive references

title: Microservices - Martin Fowler
core_insight: Microservices trade development simplicity for operational complexity; choose them only when your team and domain genuinely need independent deployability.
summary: Martin Fowler's foundational article on microservices architecture defines the pattern through nine characteristics, contrasts with monolithic alternatives, and emphasizes that microservices are a trade-off, not a universal improvement.
decision_reason: Classic reference, high credibility but well-known content with low novelty

title: The PARA Method: A Universal System for Organizing Digital Information
core_insight: PARA organizes all digital information into four categories (Projects, Areas, Resources, Archives) based on actionability, not topic.
summary: Tiago Forte's PARA method proposes organizing digital knowledge by actionability rather than topic. Projects are time-bound with goals, Areas are ongoing responsibilities, Resources are reference material, and Archives are inactive items.
decision_reason: Popular PKM framework, actionable but more opinion-based than evidence-based

title: How to Get Startup Ideas - Paul Graham
core_insight: The best startup ideas come from noticing problems you personally have, not from trying to think of startup ideas.
summary: Paul Graham argues that good startup ideas are discovered organically by working on things that interest you and noticing what's missing. Deliberately brainstorming startup ideas tends to produce bad ones.
decision_reason: Well-known essay with strong anecdotal evidence, widely referenced in startup community

title: What is Hybrid Search? Combining Keyword and Vector Search
core_insight: Hybrid search combining keyword (sparse) and semantic (dense) retrieval consistently outperforms either approach alone across diverse query types.
summary: Algolia explains how hybrid search merges traditional keyword matching with vector-based semantic search. The approach handles both precise queries and conceptual searches better than either method alone.
decision_reason: Relevant technical explainer from search company, useful reference for our search implementation
